questions.question: In XRD, why do dislocations in cold-rolled metals cause peak broadening?

answers.b: They create lattice distortions and micro-strains
answers.c: They reduce the number of diffraction planes
answers.d: They completely change the crystal structure

questions.question: Which line-broadening analysis method is commonly used to estimate dislocation density from XRD data?
In Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM), dislocations are visible because they __________.

answers.a: reflect more electrons than the surrounding matrix
answers.b: emit X-rays that are detected by the microscope
answers.c: cause local contrast differences by bending the lattice planes
answers.d: remove atoms completely from the sample

questions.question: To calculate dislocation density using a TEM micrograph, you need to know the total length of dislocation lines and __________.

answers.b: the thickness of the sample foil and the imaged area
answers.c: the energy of the electron beam
answers.d: the chemical composition of the metal

questions.question: Dislocation density is generally expressed in which unit?
In cold-rolled metals, the typical range for high dislocation densities is around __________.
To separate the true peak broadening from instrumental effects in XRD dislocation analysis, researchers typically __________.

answers.a: use the same measurement on multiple instruments
answers.b: subtract the instrumental broadening measured with a strain-free reference
answers.c: rely solely on TEM images for correction
answers.d: ignore instrumental broadening as it is negligible
